name: Single transaction limit
description: Raises an activity warning when one fiat transaction crosses the client review threshold.

name: Large single transaction
description: Fixed platform threshold for large individual transaction review.

name: Total volume review
description: Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the lower review threshold.

name: High total volume review
description: Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the high review threshold.

name: Transaction velocity
description: Raises a warning when a client creates three transactions inside a 24 hour window.

name: Wallet reputation review
description: Represents wallet reputation alerts returned by the external KYCAID risk check.

name: Sanctions Match
description: Trigger: Customer, fiat sender/beneficiary, wallet address, blockchain cluster, or counterparty matches sanctions screening. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Confirm match quality, identifiers, wallet ownership, transaction status, and whether the transaction must be rejected, frozen, or escalated.

name: High-Risk Wallet Exposure
description: Trigger: Requested payout wallet or incoming crypto wallet has exposure to darknet markets, scams, ransomware, stolen funds, sanctioned entities, terrorist financing, or other high-risk services. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check direct/indirect exposure, exposure percentage, hop distance, transaction amount, and customer explanation.

name: Mixer or Obfuscation Exposure
description: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.

name: Third-Party Fiat Funding
description: Trigger: Fiat payment comes from a bank account, card, or payment method not held in the verified customer's name. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Compare sender name with KYC name. Request proof of relationship or reject if third-party funding is prohibited.

name: Third-Party Fiat Payout
description: Trigger: Customer requests fiat payout to a bank account not held in their own verified name. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check beneficiary ownership, account name, country, and whether payout violates internal policy.

name: Same Bank Account Used by Multiple Customers
description: Trigger: Two or more unrelated customers use the same IBAN, card, payment account, or fiat sender/beneficiary details. Applies to: Fiat deposits and payouts. Review: Check shared address, device, IP, wallet address, and possible mule network activity.

name: Same Wallet Used by Multiple Customers
description: Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.

name: Multiple Fiat Sources to One Wallet
description: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.

name: Multiple Wallets to One Fiat Account
description: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.

name: Structuring Below Thresholds
description: Trigger: Customer performs several transactions just below internal review thresholds. Applies to: Fiat deposits, fiat payouts, crypto purchases, and crypto sales. Example: Three or more transactions just below EUR 1,000 / EUR 3,000 / EUR 10,000 within 24 hours or 7 days. Review: Check whether the pattern appears designed to avoid checks.

name: High-Value New Customer Transaction
description: Trigger: Newly onboarded customer performs a high-value transaction shortly after registration. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: Transaction above EUR 5,000 within 7 days of onboarding. Review: Check KYC quality, source of funds, wallet risk, payment ownership, and IP/device risk.

name: Activity Inconsistent With Customer Profile
description: Trigger: Transaction amount or frequency is inconsistent with customer occupation, income, expected activity, or previous behavior. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Request source of funds/source of wealth where needed.

name: Unusual Transaction Velocity
description: Trigger: Customer performs unusually frequent transactions in a short period. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: More than 5 transactions in 24 hours or 10 transactions in 7 days, depending on normal customer behavior. Review: Check whether activity is normal market behavior, business activity, or possible layering.

name: Rapid Repeated Wallet Changes
description: Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.

name: High-Risk Jurisdiction Indicator
description: Trigger: Customer, fiat bank, IP address, wallet service, or counterparty is linked to a high-risk or restricted jurisdiction. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Check country risk, customer residency, payment country, IP location, and sanctions restrictions.

name: VPN, Proxy, TOR, or Location Mismatch
description: Trigger: Customer initiates transaction using VPN/proxy/TOR or from a country inconsistent with KYC/residency. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check login history, device changes, travel explanation, and whether the country is restricted.

name: Crypto-to-Fiat From Risky Source Wallet
description: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.

name: Fiat-to-Crypto To Risky Destination Wallet
description: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.

name: Privacy Coin or High-Anonymity Asset Use
description: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.

name: Chain-Hopping or Asset-Hopping
description: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.

name: Bridge Exposure
description: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.

name: Travel Rule Information Missing or Incomplete
description: Trigger: Required originator/beneficiary information is missing, incomplete, inconsistent, or rejected by counterparty CASP/VASP. Applies to: Crypto transfers where Travel Rule applies. Review: Check whether counterparty is a CASP/VASP or self-hosted wallet and whether required information was collected.

name: Counterparty CASP/VASP High Risk
description: Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.

name: Payment Reversal After Crypto Delivery
description: Trigger: Fiat payment is reversed, recalled, disputed, or charged back after crypto was sent to the requested wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check fraud indicators, customer history, destination wallet, and linked accounts.

name: Repeated Cancelled or Failed Payment Attempts
description: Trigger: Customer has multiple failed cards, rejected bank transfers, name mismatches, or payment attempts. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check stolen card risk, mule activity, payment method ownership, and device/IP behavior.

name: Dormant Customer Reactivation
description: Trigger: Inactive customer suddenly performs high-value activity or uses a new bank account, new wallet, new device, or new IP country. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check account takeover risk and whether the activity fits prior customer behavior.

name: Repeated Alerts on Same Customer
description: Trigger: Customer triggers multiple AML rules within a short period. Applies to: All transactions. Example: Three or more alerts within 30 days. Review: Assess the full pattern, not only each transaction separately.

name: Source of Funds Not Supported
description: Trigger: Customer cannot provide reasonable source of funds/source of wealth for transaction size or pattern. Applies to: High-value or unusual fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat transactions. Review: Request documents such as salary evidence, bank statement, sale agreement, business revenue proof, or crypto acquisition proof.
